prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, portrait orientation
Primary request: Call of Cthulhu / 1920s Lovecraftian inspired cosmic horror atlas for the terrain type continent_archipelago.
Scene/backdrop: one dominant New England-like continent with a large uneven offshore archipelago, Arkham-style towns, Miskatonic river delta, foggy rocky hills, old colonial harbors, lighthouse coast, cult-haunted fishing villages, forbidden standing stones, marsh islands, old rail lines, shipwreck reefs and hidden ruins connected to cosmic dread.
Style/medium: high-resolution illustrated 1920s investigative atlas, aged parchment with hand annotations, sepia ink, fog-stained paper, creeping cosmic dread, occult diagram margins, subtle impossible geometry.
Composition/framing: tall portrait map; main continent off-center with an irregular archipelago sweeping along the coast in a diagonal arc; islands include tiny haunted islets, lighthouse rocks, reef belts, large fishing islands, crescent island chains, marsh islands and storm-battered atolls; include compass rose, hand-drawn border, ship routes, rail routes, lighthouse icons, standing stone symbols and investigator annotations without readable text.
Geography constraints: islands must be irregularly distributed and varied in size and shape, with channels, dark lagoons, reefs, wreck fields, atolls and broken shores; continental coastline should include peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, curved coasts, deltas, salt marshes, foggy estuaries, capes, cliff coasts and fragmented rocks; show rocky coasts, dark forests, farmlands, mountains, wetlands, cold islands, moors and uncanny wastelands.
Avoid: uniform island grid, heroic fantasy, visible monster centerpiece, simple round islands, modern satellite image, clean tourist map, readable labels, UI, watermark, logo, real-world map copy.
